channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Human excrement, 4th March
item.description:
Large amount of what appears to be human excrement on pavement in front of stand toilets not able to enter stand toilets without walking through it
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 23b, Denmark Hill, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Near the Veolia Bins, 4th March
item.description:
House good waste

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Koops Mill Mews, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pothole in road, 4th March
item.description:
Big pothole

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 79, Compayne Gardens, South Hampstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumped again including food waste, 4th March
item.description:
Time and time again rubbish is dumped here. I could report it everyday but I don't have time. Is there no way we can get more bins here or a public bin as people just dump their rubbish on top of the bags left there?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Silvester Street, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Multiple areas of dog fouling, 4th March
item.description:
I walk my dog here most days and the dog fouling in this area has become increasingly worse. This morning there were 9 different areas where faeces had not been picked up. This has been getting worse for about a year now.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Smalley Close, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocked drains Linthorpe Road N16, 4th March
item.description:
Blocked drain outside 33-35 Linthorpe Road N16 also opposite side of pavement and top of road.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Linthorpe Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping in bins area next to Barbrook house, 4th March
item.description:
There are two sofas, multiple cardboards, a bed side table, a bed frame and just general rubbish all around including bags on top of the recycling bins.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Paragon Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Linthorpe Road N16 needs cleaning, 4th March
item.description:
Linthorpe Road N16 Needs cleaning for top to bottom, around tree bases Gullies and pavements.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Linthorpe Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Deadly food waste ongoing problem, 4th March
item.description:
Last night my dog ate a discarded chicken bone and nearly died, he is only 1, and it has cost £5000 as well as health risks to deal with this. Why is this relevant for the council? This is not the first time a dog has eaten a chicken bone or something deadly along Sidcup’s streets. In fact I know several dogs in the last 12 months alone where this has happened, not to mention the discarded food such as chocolate which just litters the floor. Sidcup council obvious are aware as we have bins on the high street every few meters. However this incident occurred at the green outside the star in Sidcup near the sixth form. It is almost certainly a result of the school kids littering. We have 2 ticket wardens who patrol Sidcup all day everyday, they have unfortunately given me tickets in the past so I know exactly who they are when they walk past. Often they walk in tandem and relatively laxidaisily. So it fustrated me that we have no community support officers in Sidcup during the peak school hours. Evidently there is a wider problem then litter too, we own a flat in the storyteller above the library/cinema. Almost every day we have sixth form students congregate outside of our front door normally in groups of 4-12, and they literally stand outside of the door leaning on it. They then on an almost weekly basis they try to get into our building where our parcels / mail room is or repeat call our doorbell. I know the shops have to limit the number of them who come into their building too. Realistically the only thing that will help change behaviour now is intervention and fines. I would imagine officer or community support officers in the area over the school lunch period through to when they leave Sidcup after school c.4pm, who can issue on the spot fines for these types of behaviours, at least on a temporary basis is the only way to start addressing this now. Is there a feasible way of starting to address this that the police can support on because I am at my limit after yesterday and I am a very tolerant and understanding person.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Chislehurst Road, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Meter long half meter wide road cavity, 4th March
item.description:
Humongous hole in road. Can't call it a pot hole due to size. Failed repair very long time ago. This time for the sequel you will need a bigger boat to repair this bad boy. Will probably need its own postcode before long. The 40 odd days of rain in January have caused loads of chippings to become loose and fly up.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): New Road, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Huge crator of a pothole, 4th March
item.description:
Massive pothole open at mouth of road. Very wide and long. Stones chip up and drivers swerve round. Dangerous spot as its where people cross and traffic crosses paths.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Upper Wickham Lane, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Shopping trolley full of rubbish, 4th March
item.description:
Shopping trolley full of rubbish opposite westbere house,needs removing

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sholden Gardens, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: I saw a white Luton van anloading rubbish, 4th March
item.description:
5:35am a white Luton van unloaded loads of rubbish

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Barnfield Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: All lights not working reported by someone else too, 4th March
item.description:
All the lampposts are not working in the park which is making the park extremely dangerous to walk through after sunset now knowing sunset is at 5-6pm. I heard screaming the other day in the park and weird anti social behaviour happening when I was passing by, so I did not walk through the park and walked alongside the roads instead. Please Peabody fix this asap
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pettman Crescent, Plumstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cardboard boxes, 4th March
item.description:
Just left on the street

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): McDowall Road, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Large u shaped pot hole
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Birdbrook Road, Kidbrooke

item.title: Untaxed vehicle in parking spot, 4th March
item.description:
This car has been dumped there

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Frobisher Road, Slade Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Significant potholes, 4th March
item.description:
Multiple large potholes are present. These are bigger than usual and heavily affect safety for bicycle users - having to swerve to avoid them, potentially causing conflicts with other traffic and danger.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 29-57, Halton Road, Islington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipped furniture on Anerley Park, near nursery, 4th March
item.description:
This large green sofa has been fly tipped on the pavement between 47-49 Anerley Park for over a month. It is unsightly, and it's proximity to a nursery a few doors down could pose a risk to children passing by as the sofa has been propped up precariously.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Anerley Park, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Huge pothole, 3rd March
item.description:
Just on the roundabout. Hits the tyre hard. Will cause an accident because I see loads of people trying to avoid it despite it being a roundabout that you need to stay in lane for!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Townley Road, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
